# Test Specification: Arch AI Trace Explorer

**Feature Spec**: `docs/features/arch-trace-explorer.md`
**HLD**: `docs/specs/arch-trace-explorer.hld.md` (TBD — to be generated by `/hld`)
**LLD**: `docs/plans/<date>-arch-trace-explorer-impl-plan.md` (TBD — to be generated by `/lld`)
**Status**: PLANNED
**Last Updated**: 2026-04-15

---

## 1. Coverage Matrix

Every functional requirement from the feature spec is mapped to its test coverage. Status column tracks implementation progress.

| FR    | Description                                                                                     | Unit | Integration | E2E | Manual | Status  |
| ----- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ---- | ----------- | --- | ------ | ------- |
| FR-1  | Hierarchical spans with `traceId = sessionId`, child spans linked by `parentSpanId`             | ✅   | ✅          | ✅  | —      | PLANNED |
| FR-2  | Implements shared `Tracer` + `Span` interface (not bespoke emitter)                             | ✅   | —           | —   | —      | PLANNED |
| FR-3  | `AsyncLocalStorage` parent propagation across execution chain                                   | ✅   | ✅          | —   | —      | PLANNED |
| FR-4  | Three lifecycle events: `span_start` INSERT, `span_update` batched UPDATE, `span_end` UPSERT    | ✅   | ✅          | —   | —      | PLANNED |
| FR-5  | `span_end` upsert with `$setOnInsert` for all required scope fields                             | ✅   | ✅          | —   | —      | PLANNED |
| FR-6  | Atomic session revision via `findOneAndUpdate($inc)` + ordered `bulkWrite`                      | ✅   | ✅          | —   | —      | PLANNED |
| FR-7  | Additive `arch_*` event types in observatory; existing `llm_call`/`tool_call` reused unchanged  | ✅   | —           | —   | —      | PLANNED |
| FR-8  | `ok` → `completed` status mapping at write time inside `MongoWritePipeline`                     | ✅   | —           | —   | —      | PLANNED |
| FR-9  | Redaction boundary: secrets, PII, 4 KB truncation, no raw LLM payloads by default               | ✅   | ✅          | ✅  | —      | PLANNED |
| FR-10 | Raw-payload opt-in mode (`ARCH_TRACE_RAW_PAYLOADS=true`) with shorter TTL + `rawCapture` tag    | ✅   | ✅          | —   | —      | PLANNED |
| FR-11 | Error bubbling walks up parent chain; stops at root or first error ancestor                     | ✅   | ✅          | ✅  | —      | PLANNED |
| FR-12 | Project-scoped HTTP routes filter every query by `{ tenantId, projectId }`; cross-project → 404 | —    | ✅          | ✅  | —      | PLANNED |
| FR-13 | Onboarding-scoped routes filter by `{ tenantId, userId, projectId: null }`; cross-user → 404    | —    | ✅          | ✅  | —      | PLANNED |
| FR-14 | MongoDB indexes split by scope (project + onboarding); TTL index on `expiresAt`                 | ✅   | —           | —   | —      | PLANNED |
| FR-15 | LLM span attributes captured from Vercel AI SDK `onStepFinish` at every call site               | ✅   | ✅          | ✅  | —      | PLANNED |
| FR-16 | Tool span attributes (`tool.name`, `callId`, `resultStatus`, `retryCount`, summaries)           | ✅   | ✅          | —   | —      | PLANNED |
| FR-17 | Root span name backfilled from first user message; session `name` updated atomically            | —    | ✅          | ✅  | —      | PLANNED |
| FR-18 | Master-detail UI (session list + tree + detail panel); auto-poll every 5 s while active         | —    | —           | ✅  | ✅     | PLANNED |
| FR-19 | Type-specific span detail panels (LLM/Tool/Phase transition) with breadcrumb and expandables    | —    | —           | ✅  | ✅     | PLANNED |
| FR-20 | `arch:traces:read` permission enforced on project routes only; onboarding gated by user-scope   | —    | ✅          | —   | —      | PLANNED |
| FR-21 | Tenant-deletion cascade removes `ArchTraceSpan` and `ArchTraceSession` for that tenant          | ✅   | ✅          | —   | —      | PLANNED |
| FR-22 | 2,000-span session cap; 500-span poll cap with continuation revision                            | ✅   | ✅          | —   | —      | PLANNED |
| FR-23 | UI gated behind `NEXT_PUBLIC_FEATURE_ARCH_TRACE_EXPLORER`; legacy tab when off                  | —    | —           | ✅  | ✅     | PLANNED |

**Totals**: 23 FRs; 16 unit-covered, 15 integration-covered, 10 E2E-covered, 4 manual-covered. No FR is untested.

---

## 2. E2E Test Scenarios (MANDATORY)

> **CRITICAL**: E2E tests exercise the real system through its HTTP API. No `vi.mock()` of platform packages. No direct DB queries in assertions. Real MongoDB (dev instance for Playwright; `MongoMemoryServer` for vitest in-process E2E). Full middleware chain executes: auth, tenant isolation, project access, permission check, feature-flag gates.
>
> **Two tiers** (both canonical in this repo):
>
> 1. **Vitest in-process E2E** — `apps/studio/src/__tests__/e2e/arch-trace-explorer.e2e.test.ts` — boots Next.js route handlers directly with `NextRequest`, uses `MongoMemoryServer` via `packages/database/src/__tests__/helpers/setup-mongo.ts`. Fast, deterministic; covers scope enforcement, redaction, span-cap.
> 2. **Playwright browser E2E** — `apps/studio/e2e/arch-trace-explorer.spec.ts` — full browser against real dev Studio + dev MongoDB, reuses `apps/studio/e2e/helpers/` (`loginViaDevApi`, `apiGet/Post`, `pollUntil`, `screenshot`). Covers UI interactions, feature-flag toggle, auto-poll visual behaviour.
>
> **Seeding strategy**: A dedicated test-only `POST /api/arch-ai/traces/_seed` endpoint guarded by `process.env.NODE_ENV === 'test'` + admin role (mirrors existing `apps/studio/src/app/api/arch-ai/audit-logs/_seed/route.ts`). The endpoint writes through the **real** `ArchTracer` → `ArchRedactionBoundary` → `MongoWritePipeline` so that FR-6 (revision), FR-9 (redaction), and FR-22 (span cap) are exercised from seeded data. Direct `insertMany` into `arch_trace_spans` is **forbidden** in E2E tests — it would bypass the pipeline and produce false passes on FR-9 and FR-6.

### E2E-1: Full Session Trace Tree Renders (Tier: Playwright)

- **Preconditions**: Real dev MongoDB running. Feature flag `NEXT_PUBLIC_FEATURE_ARCH_TRACE_EXPLORER=true`. Seed via a **real Arch AI session** using a stub LLM client injected through DI (invokes `onStepFinish` deterministically with `inputTokens=250, outputTokens=180, finishReason='stop', model='gpt-4o-mini'`) and a stub tool executor (returns `{ success: true, output: 'mock tool result' }`).
- **Steps**:
  1. Authenticate as workspace MEMBER with `arch:traces:read` on a seeded project (`project-e2e-1`) via `loginViaDevApi`.
  2. `POST /api/arch-ai/sessions` with `{ projectId: 'project-e2e-1' }` → assert `sessionId` returned, root span created in MongoDB with `name='New Session'` and `status='running'` (verified by calling GET route, not direct DB).
  3. `POST /api/arch-ai/message` with a prompt `"Build a customer service bot"`; wait for SSE stream close (stub LLM resolves in ~200 ms with one tool call and a final text response).
  4. `GET /api/projects/project-e2e-1/arch-ai/traces/sessions` → assert response contains the session card with `name='Build a customer service bot'` (truncated to ≤ 50 chars from first user message) and `spanCount ≥ 5`.
  5. Navigate to `/arch-settings/traces` in Studio UI.
  6. Click the session card → assert the trace tree renders all **5 levels**: `session → phase → turn → llm_call → tool_execution`. Each level must have non-null `startTime` and `endTime` and `status='completed'`.
  7. Click the `llm_call` span node → assert detail panel shows `llm.model='gpt-4o-mini'`, `llm.inputTokens=250`, `llm.outputTokens=180`, `llm.totalTokens=430`, `llm.finishReason='stop'`, and `llm.estimatedCost` is a non-null positive number.
  8. Click the `tool_execution` span node → assert detail panel shows `tool.name`, `tool.resultStatus='success'`, `tool.inputSummary` contains `[REDACTED]` if the stub tool was given a synthetic secret, and `tool.durationMs > 0`.
  9. Capture a screenshot to `apps/studio/e2e/screenshots/arch-trace-explorer-tree.png` for visual regression reference.
- **Expected Result**: All 5 span levels render; LLM metrics populated from `onStepFinish`; tool metrics populated from tool-executor instrumentation; session name backfilled from first user message (proves FR-1, FR-15, FR-16, FR-17).
- **Auth Context**: Workspace MEMBER with `arch:traces:read`, project `project-e2e-1`, tenant `tenant-e2e-1`.
- **Isolation Check**: `GET /api/projects/project-other/arch-ai/traces/sessions` → **404** (different project, not in user's access set).

### E2E-2: Error Bubbling Visible in UI (Tier: Playwright)

- **Preconditions**: Same setup as E2E-1, but stub tool executor configured to **throw** `new Error('Tool timeout after 10s')` on invocation. Feature flag on.
- **Steps**:
  1. Authenticate as workspace MEMBER.
  2. `POST /api/arch-ai/sessions` + `POST /api/arch-ai/message` triggering the failing tool.
  3. Wait for session to reach terminal state (`DONE` or `ERROR`) via polling `GET /api/arch-ai/sessions/:id/status`.
  4. Navigate to `/arch-settings/traces` and click the session card.
  5. Assert session card shows the **red ERROR status badge** (not green COMPLETED).
  6. Expand the tree — assert **every ancestor** of the failed tool call shows an error indicator: phase span red dot, turn span red dot, llm_call span red dot, tool_execution span red dot with an error-banner underlay.
  7. Click the tool span → assert detail panel renders an **error banner** showing the error message `"Tool timeout after 10s"`, an error code (if set by the tracer), and a truncated stack trace (scrubbed of file paths beyond the first 3 frames).
  8. Assert the next sibling `llm_call` span (if a retry occurred) is rendered as a **separate branch** and may have `status='completed'` — the error does not "infect" sibling spans.
- **Expected Result**: Error status propagates from tool up through llm_call → turn → phase → session; every ancestor is visibly red; sibling spans are independent (proves FR-11).
- **Auth Context**: Workspace MEMBER with `arch:traces:read` on `project-e2e-2`.
- **Isolation Check**: N/A — same-project scenario.

### E2E-3: Live Polling Shows In-Flight Span (Tier: Playwright)

- **Preconditions**: Feature flag on. Stub tool executor configured with a 10-second artificial `await new Promise(r => setTimeout(r, 10_000))` to guarantee the tool span stays in `running` state across poll cycles.
- **Steps**:
  1. Authenticate as workspace MEMBER.
  2. `POST /api/arch-ai/sessions` + `POST /api/arch-ai/message` (non-blocking; return as soon as SSE stream opens).
  3. Immediately navigate to `/arch-settings/traces`. The session card should be visible in left panel with `ACTIVE` status.
  4. Click the session — trace tree renders with the tool span in `running` state (blue pulsing dot).
  5. Start a **network request recorder** (via Playwright `page.route()`) that captures every `GET .../poll?sinceRevision=*` request and its response body.
  6. Wait 6 seconds — assert at least **one** poll response contained the running span with `status='running'` and `endTime=null`.
  7. Wait for the stub tool's 10-second delay to elapse.
  8. Assert a subsequent poll response contained the **same** `spanId` with `status='completed'` and `endTime != null` and `durationMs ≥ 10000`.
  9. Assert the tree node visually transitioned from blue pulsing → green solid dot (DOM class assertion, not screenshot).
- **Expected Result**: Live polling captures running-then-completed lifecycle; UI transitions without flicker (proves FR-4, FR-6, FR-18 auto-poll).
- **Auth Context**: Workspace MEMBER with `arch:traces:read` on `project-e2e-3`.
- **Isolation Check**: N/A.

### E2E-4: Cross-Scope Access Returns 404 (Tier: Vitest in-process)

- **Preconditions**: `MongoMemoryServer` started per §3 harness. Two tenants (`tenant-A`, `tenant-B`). Tenant-A has one project `project-A` and one user `user-A1`; Tenant-B has `project-B` and `user-B1`. Seed via `_seed` endpoint:
  - One IN_PROJECT session for `tenant-A / project-A / user-A1` with 3 spans
  - One ONBOARDING session for `tenant-A / user-A1` with 3 spans (`projectId: null`)
  - One IN_PROJECT session for `tenant-B / project-B / user-B1` with 3 spans
- **Steps**:
  1. Authenticate as `user-A1`.
  2. `GET /api/projects/project-A/arch-ai/traces/sessions` → assert 200 + one session in response.
  3. `GET /api/projects/project-B/arch-ai/traces/sessions` → assert **404** (different tenant + different project; `requireProject` blocks before `requirePermission`).
  4. `GET /api/projects/project-A/arch-ai/traces/sessions/<sessionIdFromTenantB>` → assert **404**.
  5. `GET /api/projects/project-A/arch-ai/traces/spans/<spanIdFromTenantB>` → assert **404** (scope filter in query rejects, not post-verify leakage).
  6. `GET /api/projects/project-A/arch-ai/traces/sessions/<ownSessionId>/poll?sinceRevision=0` → assert 200.
  7. `GET /api/projects/project-A/arch-ai/traces/sessions/<sessionIdFromTenantB>/poll?sinceRevision=0` → assert **404**.
  8. `GET /api/arch-ai/traces/onboarding/sessions` as `user-A1` → assert one session (own onboarding), not the IN_PROJECT one.
  9. Authenticate as `user-B1` (different user, different tenant).
  10. `GET /api/arch-ai/traces/onboarding/sessions/<onboardingSessionIdFromA>` → assert **404**.
  11. Authenticate as a new `user-A2` inside `tenant-A` (same tenant, different user).
  12. `GET /api/arch-ai/traces/onboarding/sessions/<onboardingSessionIdFromA1>` → assert **404** (cross-user within tenant is still private).
  13. `GET /api/projects/project-A/arch-ai/traces/sessions` as `user-A2` → assert 200 (project-scoped is shared within tenant, not user-private).
- **Expected Result**: Every cross-scope access returns **404** (never 403, never 200 with empty list, never leaks existence). Same-scope access within permission grants returns 200 (proves FR-12, FR-13, FR-20).
- **Auth Context**: Two tenants × two users each = 4 auth contexts exercised.
- **Isolation Check**: This IS the isolation test.

### E2E-5: Feature Flag Toggles UI (Tier: Playwright)

- **Preconditions**: Real dev Studio with feature flag configurable at boot via env var. Admin credentials.
- **Steps**:
  1. Start Studio with `NEXT_PUBLIC_FEATURE_ARCH_TRACE_EXPLORER=false`.
  2. Authenticate and navigate to `/arch-settings`.
  3. Assert the **"Audit Logs"** tab is visible (legacy `ArchAuditLogsTab` component rendered).
  4. Click the tab → assert a flat log table renders (not a trace tree).
  5. Assert **no** "Traces" tab exists (feature flag OFF hides it).
  6. Capture screenshot: `arch-trace-explorer-flag-off.png`.
  7. Restart Studio with `NEXT_PUBLIC_FEATURE_ARCH_TRACE_EXPLORER=true`.
  8. Navigate to `/arch-settings`.
  9. Assert the **"Traces"** tab is visible (new `TraceExplorer` component).
  10. Click the tab → assert master-detail layout renders (left session list, right tree + detail panel).
  11. Assert **no** "Audit Logs" tab exists (replaced by Traces per feature spec §2 Goals).
  12. Capture screenshot: `arch-trace-explorer-flag-on.png`.
- **Expected Result**: Flag toggles the tab and its underlying component cleanly (proves FR-23).
- **Auth Context**: Workspace OWNER (to see arch settings).
- **Isolation Check**: N/A — rollout mechanic test.

### E2E-6: Redaction Scrubs Secrets from Stored Spans (Tier: Vitest in-process)

- **Preconditions**: `MongoMemoryServer` started. `ARCH_TRACE_RAW_PAYLOADS=false` (default). Seed via `POST /api/arch-ai/traces/_seed` with a tool span whose input contains three synthetic secret patterns:
  - Synthetic OpenAI-like key: `sk-test_ABC123XYZ456DEF789GHI012JKL345MNO678PQR`
  - Synthetic JWT: `Bearer eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJ0ZXN0IjoidGVzdCJ9.test_signature`
  - Synthetic email PII: `user@example.com`
  - Plain non-secret text: `"Hello Arch"` (must pass through unchanged)
- **Steps**:
  1. Authenticate as test admin.
  2. `POST /api/arch-ai/traces/_seed` with the tool span payload.
  3. `GET /api/projects/:projectId/arch-ai/traces/spans/:toolSpanId` → inspect `attributes['tool.inputSummary']`.
  4. Assert the response **does NOT** contain the raw secret patterns (no `sk-test_ABC...`, no `eyJhbGc...`).
  5. Assert the response **does** contain `[REDACTED]` markers where secrets were.
  6. Assert `user@example.com` is replaced by `[REDACTED_EMAIL]` (PII handler).
  7. Assert `"Hello Arch"` is preserved verbatim in the summary.
  8. Assert `attributes['trace.rawCapture']` is either absent or `'false'` (default mode, not raw).
  9. Re-run the test with `ARCH_TRACE_RAW_PAYLOADS=true`:
     - Seed the same span through a new `_seed` call.
     - Assert the stored span now has `attributes['trace.rawCapture'] = 'true'`.
     - Assert secret patterns are **still** redacted (raw mode does NOT disable scrubbing; it only disables the 4 KB truncation).
     - Assert full text beyond 4 KB is preserved (not truncated).
- **Expected Result**: Default mode scrubs secrets + PII + truncates to 4 KB; raw mode preserves full text but still scrubs secrets (proves FR-9, FR-10).
- **Auth Context**: Test admin for `_seed`, workspace MEMBER for read queries.
- **Isolation Check**: N/A — single-tenant test.

---

## 3. Integration Test Scenarios (MANDATORY)

> **CRITICAL**: Integration tests exercise real service boundaries. `vi.mock()` of `@agent-platform/*`, `@abl/*`, or relative imports is **forbidden**. Only external third-party packages (`ai`, `openai`) may be stubbed via dependency injection.
>
> **MongoDB harness**: Integration suites import `MongoMemoryServer` directly from `mongodb-memory-server` and follow the existing Studio pattern (see `apps/studio/src/__tests__/e2e/arch-ai-sessions.e2e.test.ts`). The canonical helper `packages/database/src/__tests__/helpers/setup-mongo.ts` is **internal** to `@agent-platform/database` — its subpath is not re-exported from the package today. Tests outside the database package must either (a) instantiate `MongoMemoryServer` directly in a local `beforeAll`, or (b) the LLD adds a `./testing` subpath export to `packages/database/package.json` and tests import from there. **This test spec assumes pattern (a)** until the LLD decides otherwise. Each suite must handle the "mongodb-memory-server binary failed to download" case by skipping (not hanging) — mirror the `beforeAll(async () => { try { mongod = await MongoMemoryServer.create(); } catch { return skip(); } })` pattern from existing Studio E2E tests.

### INT-1: Project-Scoped Routes Enforce `{ tenantId, projectId }` in Every Query

- **Boundary**: HTTP → `withRouteHandler({ requireProject, permissions: StudioPermission.ARCH_TRACES_READ })` → MongoDB query
- **File**: `apps/studio/src/__tests__/arch-ai/traces-project-scoped.integration.test.ts`
- **Setup**:
  - MongoMemoryServer per §3 harness in `beforeAll`
  - **Compile-time anchor** — assert `StudioPermission.ARCH_TRACES_READ === 'arch:traces:read'` as the first statement of the suite (couples this test spec to FR-20 delivery; if the constant is not exported from `apps/studio/src/lib/permissions.ts`, the suite fails at import time)
  - Seed two tenants × two projects each (4 projects total) with 5 spans per project via `POST /api/arch-ai/traces/_seed`
  - Register `arch:traces:read` permission; grant it to the test user on `tenant-A/project-A` only
- **Steps**: 0. **Unauthenticated**: `GET /api/projects/project-A/arch-ai/traces/sessions` with no Authorization header → **401** (covers `withRouteHandler` default auth step)
  1. `GET /api/projects/project-A/arch-ai/traces/sessions` as user-A → 200, only `project-A` sessions
  2. `GET /api/projects/project-A/arch-ai/traces/spans/<spanFromProjectB>` → **404** (`requireProject` rejects before query, no existence leakage)
  3. `GET /api/projects/project-A/arch-ai/traces/sessions/<sessionFromProjectB>` → **404**
  4. `GET /api/projects/project-A/arch-ai/traces/sessions/<ownSessionId>/poll?sinceRevision=0` → 200
  5. `GET /api/projects/project-A/arch-ai/traces/sessions/<sessionFromProjectB>/poll?sinceRevision=0` → **404**
  6. Create a second user `user-A2` in the same tenant WITHOUT `arch:traces:read` grant: `GET /api/projects/project-A/arch-ai/traces/sessions` → **403** (project access granted via tenant, but permission not granted — covers the 403 branch of `withRouteHandler`)
  7. Cross-tenant: `GET /api/projects/project-A/arch-ai/traces/sessions` as `user-B1` → **404**
  8. Malformed query param: `GET /api/projects/project-A/arch-ai/traces/sessions/<ownSessionId>/poll?sinceRevision=not-a-number` → **400** (validation)
- **Expected Result**: Cross-project/cross-tenant always 404; same-project no-permission is 403; unauthenticated is 401; malformed input is 400 (proves FR-12, FR-20).
- **Failure Mode**: If `withRouteHandler` ever regresses to run permission before project access, step 2 would return 403 instead of 404, leaking project existence.

### INT-2: Onboarding Routes Enforce `{ tenantId, userId, projectId: null }`

- **Boundary**: HTTP → `withRouteHandler({})` (default auth) → MongoDB query
- **File**: `apps/studio/src/__tests__/arch-ai/traces-onboarding-scoped.integration.test.ts`
- **Setup**:
  - MongoMemoryServer per §3 harness
  - Seed two users in the same tenant with 3 onboarding spans each (both with `projectId: null`)
  - Seed one IN_PROJECT session for `user-A` (with `projectId != null`)
- **Steps**:
  1. `GET /api/arch-ai/traces/onboarding/sessions` as user-A → returns only user-A's onboarding sessions (1 session), not the IN_PROJECT one
  2. `GET /api/arch-ai/traces/onboarding/sessions/<userBOnboardingId>` as user-A → **404**
  3. `GET /api/arch-ai/traces/onboarding/spans/<spanFromUserB>` as user-A → **404**
  4. `GET /api/arch-ai/traces/onboarding/sessions/<userAInProjectSessionId>` as user-A → **404** (project-scoped session is not accessible via onboarding route because query filter includes `projectId: null`)
  5. Cross-user within same tenant: `GET /api/arch-ai/traces/onboarding/sessions/<userAOnboardingId>` as user-B → **404**
- **Expected Result**: Onboarding routes are user-private within a tenant; project-scoped sessions are invisible here (proves FR-13).
- **Failure Mode**: If the onboarding reader forgets the `projectId: null` predicate, IN_PROJECT sessions would leak into the onboarding list.

### INT-3: Incremental Poll Returns Changed Spans + Ancestors in Revision Order

- **Boundary**: `MongoTraceReader.pollSince()` → MongoDB
- **File**: `apps/studio/src/__tests__/arch-ai/traces-poll.integration.test.ts`
- **Setup**:
  - MongoMemoryServer started per the §3 harness pattern
  - Seed a session with parent span `S0` and child span `S1`, both completed, via sequential `_seed` calls — revisions are assigned by the counter, so `S0.revision = r0`, `S1.revision = r1`, with `r1 > r0` (relative ordering, not absolute values)
- **Steps**:
  1. Record the current session revision as `r_before` (via a `GET .../poll?sinceRevision=0` to capture max revision)
  2. Trigger an error on `S1` via the `_seed` endpoint's `updateStatus` sub-action (defined in §7 Data seeding — accepts `{ spanId, status: 'error', errorMessage }` and writes through the real tracer, producing new revisions for `S1` and then a bubbled-update to `S0`)
  3. `GET .../poll?sinceRevision=r_before` → response contains exactly `[S1 (rev r2), S0 (rev r3)]` with `r2 > r_before` and `r3 > r2`
  4. Assert the parent span (`S0`) is caught even though its original INSERT was at `r0` — because bubbling wrote a new revision `r3`
  5. `GET .../poll?sinceRevision=r3` → response is empty
  6. Assert ordering invariant: for every consecutive pair `(a, b)` in the response, `a.revision < b.revision`
  7. Assert status invariant: `S1.status === 'error'` and `S0.status === 'error'` in the response
- **Expected Result**: Poll cursor is monotonic; error bubbles propagate to parents and are caught by incremental polls; no duplicates (proves FR-6, FR-11).
- **Failure Mode**: If `bulkWrite({ ordered: false })` was used instead of `ordered: true`, revision N+1 could commit before N, breaking the polling guarantee.

### INT-4: `span_end` Upserts When `span_start` Was Not Written

- **Boundary**: `MongoWritePipeline.flush()` → MongoDB (`updateOne` with `upsert: true`)
- **File**: `packages/arch-ai/src/tracing/__tests__/mongo-write-pipeline.test.ts`
- **Setup**: MongoMemoryServer per §3 harness; create a fresh pipeline instance
- **`startTime` derivation rule (pinned here so HLD/LLD inherit)**: When `span_end` upserts a new document because `span_start` was dropped, `startTime` MUST be computed as `endTime - durationMs` if `durationMs` is present; otherwise `startTime = endTime`. This is a **firm expectation** for FR-5 — the HLD must honour it.
- **Steps**:
  1. Construct a `span_end` event directly (without any prior `span_start`) containing final `status='completed'`, `endTime`, `durationMs=1500`, `traceId`, `parentSpanId`, `name`, and full scope fields (`tenantId`, `userId`, `sessionId`, `projectId`)
  2. Call `pipeline.write(endEvent)` and `await pipeline.flush()`
  3. Query `ArchTraceSpanModel.findOne({ spanId })` directly (unit test only — E2E would go through HTTP)
  4. Assert the document exists with all `$setOnInsert` fields populated: `traceId`, `parentSpanId`, `name`, `startTime = endTime − 1500ms` (per the rule above), `status='completed'`, `endTime`, `durationMs=1500`, scope fields, `revision` from counter, `expiresAt = now + 90d` (default mode)
  5. Repeat with `durationMs` omitted — assert `startTime === endTime`
  6. Assert a subsequent `span_update` for the same `spanId` only modifies `$set` fields, not `$setOnInsert` fields (idempotent upsert — `startTime` set on first insert is not re-derived on later updates)
- **Expected Result**: Dropped `span_start` does not cause data loss; span appears at completion with all required fields, `startTime` derived deterministically (proves FR-5).
- **Failure Mode**: If `$setOnInsert` is missing any scope field (e.g., `projectId`), the route handler's scope filter would fail to find the span on subsequent reads.

### INT-5: Redaction Scrubs Before Storage

- **Boundary**: `ArchRedactionBoundary.write()` → `WritePipeline.write()` → MongoDB
- **File**: `packages/arch-ai/src/tracing/__tests__/redaction.test.ts`
- **Setup**: MongoMemoryServer per §3 harness; create `ArchRedactionBoundary` wrapping a real `MongoWritePipeline`
- **Steps**:
  1. Write a span with tool input containing secret patterns (`sk-test_...`, PEM-like block) and PII (`user@example.com`, `+1-555-1234`)
  2. Flush
  3. Query the stored span (unit test — direct model query allowed)
  4. Assert `attributes['tool.inputSummary']` contains `[REDACTED]` (not `sk-test_...`) and `[REDACTED_EMAIL]` (not the literal email)
  5. Write a 10 KB tool output; assert stored output is truncated to ≤ 4 KB with a `…` or `[truncated]` marker
  6. Set `ARCH_TRACE_RAW_PAYLOADS=true`; write the same 10 KB output; assert:
     - Full (but still scrubbed) text is stored
     - `attributes['trace.rawCapture'] = 'true'`
     - `expiresAt` on the document is `now + 7d` (raw TTL), not `now + 90d`
- **Expected Result**: Default mode scrubs + truncates; raw mode preserves full scrubbed text with shorter TTL (proves FR-9, FR-10).
- **Failure Mode**: If redaction fails open (boundary throws, span writes raw), secrets leak. Paired with INT-5b below.

### INT-5b: Redaction Failure Is Isolated, Does Not Break Emission

- **Boundary**: `ArchRedactionBoundary.write()` — fault-injection unit-ish integration test
- **File**: `packages/arch-ai/src/tracing/__tests__/redaction-isolation.test.ts`
- **Setup**: Inject a scrubber stub that throws on a specific sentinel input
- **Steps**:
  1. Write a span whose attributes include the sentinel
  2. Flush
  3. Assert the span **was written** to MongoDB (emission did not fail)
  4. Assert the span's stored attributes have the sentinel **fully redacted to a safe fallback** (e.g., entire attribute value replaced with `[REDACTION_FAILED]`) rather than raw leakage
  5. Assert a warning log was emitted (`createLogger('arch-ai:tracing')` called with `redaction_failed` context)
- **Expected Result**: Redaction failure fails **closed** (data redacted to a safe fallback, not leaked raw); emission proceeds; incident is logged (proves FR-9 safety guarantee).
- **Failure Mode**: Regression where a scrubber exception causes the entire span to be silently dropped or written with raw values.

### INT-6: Revision Counter Is Atomic Under Sequential Flushes

- **Boundary**: `ArchTraceSessionModel.findOneAndUpdate($inc)` + `bulkWrite({ ordered: true })`
- **File**: `packages/arch-ai/src/tracing/__tests__/mongo-write-pipeline.test.ts`
- **Setup**: MongoMemoryServer per §3 harness; one shared session document
- **Steps**:
  1. Simulate two **sequential** flush cycles in the same test, each claiming 10 revisions
  2. Flush 1 claims revisions 1–10; flush 2 claims 11–20
  3. Assert the two batches have **non-overlapping** revision ranges (no duplicates)
  4. Assert ordered `bulkWrite` commits span N before N+1: inspect `_id` monotonicity (each span's `_id` ObjectId should have strictly increasing timestamp for revisions 1→10 within flush 1)
  5. Assert the session document's `revision` field is 20 after both flushes
  6. **Non-invariant case** (single-writer guarantee holds): do NOT test concurrent cross-session flushes — architecturally impossible per feature spec §7
- **Expected Result**: Revision counter is monotonic and non-duplicated under sequential flushes; bulkWrite order holds (proves FR-6).
- **Failure Mode**: Loss of monotonicity would cause polling with `$gt: sinceRevision` to skip or repeat spans.

### INT-7: LLM Span Attributes Captured from Both `onStepFinish` Call Sites

- **Boundary**: `VercelLLMStreamClient` + `ArchTracer` → `MongoWritePipeline` → MongoDB
- **File**: `apps/studio/src/__tests__/arch-ai/traces-llm-instrument.integration.test.ts`
- **Setup**:
  - MongoMemoryServer per §3 harness
  - Stub LLM client injected via DI that invokes `onStepFinish` with deterministic tokens: `{ inputTokens: 150, outputTokens: 75, totalTokens: 225, finishReason: 'stop', model: 'gpt-4o-mini' }`
  - Run both an ONBOARDING flow (via `startStream()`) and an IN_PROJECT BUILD flow (via `streamText()`) — **regression guard**: predecessor only wired onboarding
- **Steps**:
  1. Onboarding flow: `POST /api/arch-ai/message` in onboarding context; fetch the `llm_call` span via `GET /api/arch-ai/traces/onboarding/spans/:id`; assert all 6 attributes present + correct
  2. In-project BUILD flow: `POST /api/arch-ai/message` with project context that triggers BUILD phase; fetch the BUILD-path `llm_call` span via `GET /api/projects/:projectId/arch-ai/traces/spans/:id`; assert all 6 attributes present + correct
  3. Assert `llm.estimatedCost` is a positive number computed by `estimateCost('gpt-4o-mini', 150, 75)` (use `@agent-platform/shared-kernel` — never a local pricing map)
  4. Assert the span's `parentSpanId` points to the turn span (hierarchy intact)
- **Expected Result**: Both call sites emit `llm_call` spans with complete attributes; no call site is silently skipping instrumentation (proves FR-15; closes predecessor regression).
- **Failure Mode**: Predecessor arch-audit-logs shipped ALPHA with BUILD `streamText()` NOT instrumented. This test is the regression guard.

### INT-8: Phase-Transition Instrumentation

- **Boundary**: `transitionPhase()` in `packages/arch-ai/src/coordinator/phase-machine.ts:95` → `ArchTracer.addEvent()` + `ArchTracer.startSpan(name: 'phase')`
- **File**: `packages/arch-ai/src/tracing/__tests__/phase-transition.integration.test.ts`
- **Setup**:
  - MongoMemoryServer per §3 harness
  - Construct a session in `INTERVIEW` phase
  - Wire a real `ArchTracer` via DI
- **Steps**:
  1. Call `transitionPhase(session, 'BLUEPRINT')`
  2. Flush tracer
  3. Assert one `phase` span was created with `name='BLUEPRINT'` and `startTime` ≈ transition time
  4. Assert the prior phase span (`INTERVIEW`) was ended with `status='completed'` and `endTime` = transition time
  5. Assert exactly one `arch_phase_transition` event exists on the new phase span with attributes `{ from: 'INTERVIEW', to: 'BLUEPRINT', trigger: <whatever invoked transition> }`
  6. Call `transitionPhase(session, 'BUILD')` and assert another transition event chain
- **Expected Result**: Every phase change emits a bracketed phase span + `arch_phase_transition` event (proves FR-7 for phase events; closes predecessor deferred instrumentation).
- **Failure Mode**: Predecessor arch-audit-logs never instrumented `phase-transition.ts`. This regression test blocks silent skips.

### INT-9: 2,000-Span Cap and 500-Span Poll Cap

- **Boundary**: `ArchTracer.startSpan()` cap enforcement; `MongoTraceReader.pollSince()` cap + continuation
- **File**: `apps/studio/src/__tests__/arch-ai/traces-caps.integration.test.ts`
- **Setup**: MongoMemoryServer per §3 harness
- **Steps** (span cap):
  1. Seed 1,999 spans for one session via `_seed`
  2. Call tracer to emit a 2,000th span → succeeds
  3. Call tracer to emit a 2,001st span → suppressed; assert exactly one `arch_system_event` span exists with `status='error'` and `attributes['arch.systemEvent'] = 'span_cap_exceeded'`
  4. Call tracer to emit a 2,002nd span → **no** new span appears (drop, don't re-emit the cap event); total span count remains 2,001 (the original 2,000 + the one cap-exceeded event)
- **Steps** (poll cap): 5. Seed a session with 600 spans all with `revision > 0` 6. `GET .../poll?sinceRevision=0` → response contains exactly 500 spans + a `nextRevision` field pointing to the 500th span's revision 7. `GET .../poll?sinceRevision=<nextRevision>` → response contains the remaining 100 spans + `nextRevision=null` or absent
- **Expected Result**: Span cap limits session size without data loss for prior spans; poll cap chunks large deltas with continuation (proves FR-22).
- **Failure Mode**: Without the cap, a runaway session could emit millions of spans and blow up MongoDB storage.

### INT-10: Tenant-Deletion Cascade Removes Both Collections

- **Boundary**: Tenant deletion hook → `ArchTraceSpan.deleteMany({ tenantId })` + `ArchTraceSession.deleteMany({ tenantId })`
- **File**: `packages/database/src/__tests__/tenant-deletion-cascade.integration.test.ts`
- **Setup**: MongoMemoryServer per §3 harness; seed two tenants with 5 spans + 1 session doc each
- **Steps**:
  1. Invoke the cascade hook for `tenant-A`
  2. Query `ArchTraceSpanModel.find({ tenantId: 'tenant-A' })` → empty
  3. Query `ArchTraceSessionModel.find({ tenantId: 'tenant-A' })` → empty
  4. Query `ArchTraceSpanModel.find({ tenantId: 'tenant-B' })` → 5 docs (unaffected)
  5. Query `ArchTraceSessionModel.find({ tenantId: 'tenant-B' })` → 1 doc (unaffected)
- **Expected Result**: Cascade scoped to tenant; other tenants untouched (proves FR-21; closes predecessor deferred cascade).
- **Failure Mode**: Predecessor never implemented this cascade. Without it, tenant deletion leaves orphaned trace data forever.

---

## 4. Unit Test Scenarios

Unit tests cover pure functions, state machines, and isolated logic testable without any IO.

### UT-1: `ArchTracer.startSpan` Propagates Parent via AsyncLocalStorage

- **Module**: `packages/arch-ai/src/tracing/arch-tracer.ts`
- **Input**: Call `tracer.startSpan('parent')`; inside its callback, call `tracer.startSpan('child')`
- **Expected Output**: `child.parentSpanId === parent.spanId`; `child.traceId === parent.traceId`
- **File**: `packages/arch-ai/src/tracing/__tests__/arch-tracer.test.ts`

### UT-2: `span.end()` Is Idempotent (Double-End Is No-Op + Warning)

- **Module**: `ArchSpanImpl`
- **Input**: `span.end()` twice
- **Expected Output**: First call sets `endTime` and emits `span_end`; second call is a no-op, logs warning, does not re-emit
- **File**: `packages/arch-ai/src/tracing/__tests__/arch-tracer.test.ts`

### UT-3: Error Bubbling Stops at Root or First Error-State Ancestor

- **Module**: `ArchTracer` + `ArchSpanImpl.setStatus('error')`
- **Input**: Tree: `root (ok) → A (ok) → B (ok) → C (error)`. Call `C.setStatus('error'); C.end()`
- **Expected Output**: `root`, `A`, `B` all upgraded to `error`. If `A` was already `error`, stop there.
- **File**: `packages/arch-ai/src/tracing/__tests__/error-bubbling.test.ts`

### UT-3b: Bubbling to Already-Ended Parent Is Non-Throwing

- **Module**: `ArchTracer`
- **Input**: End parent first, then set child status to error
- **Expected Output**: No throw; parent status upgrade is attempted but either no-op (parent already ended with its own state) or logged as late bubble
- **File**: `packages/arch-ai/src/tracing/__tests__/error-bubbling.test.ts`

### UT-4: Redaction Scrubs Known Patterns; Non-Secret Strings Pass Through

- **Module**: `ArchRedactionBoundary`
- **Input**: Attributes containing `sk-test_XXX`, `user@example.com`, `Hello world`
- **Expected Output**: First two scrubbed; `Hello world` unchanged
- **File**: `packages/arch-ai/src/tracing/__tests__/redaction.test.ts`

### UT-5: `ok` → `completed` Status Mapping at Write Time

- **Module**: `MongoWritePipeline` (pure function extracted: `mapWriteStatusToReadStatus(write: 'ok' | 'error'): 'completed' | 'error'`)
- **Input**: `'ok'`, `'error'`
- **Expected Output**: `'completed'`, `'error'`. `'running'` is set at `span_start` (never via this function).
- **File**: `packages/arch-ai/src/tracing/__tests__/status-mapping.test.ts`

### UT-6: Pricing Map Covers All Models Referenced in Arch Settings

- **Module**: `@agent-platform/shared-kernel/model-pricing.ts` — `estimateCost(model, inputTokens, outputTokens)`
- **Input**: Every model ID from `ModelResolutionService`'s known list (`gpt-4o`, `gpt-4o-mini`, `claude-3-5-sonnet`, `claude-3-5-haiku`, etc.)
- **Expected Output**: Non-null, positive cost for every known model; `null` (not throw) for unknown model
- **File**: `packages/shared-kernel/src/__tests__/model-pricing.test.ts` (new or additive to existing)

### UT-7: Revision Counter Returns Monotonic Values Under Sequential Claims

- **Module**: `ArchTraceSessionModel.findOneAndUpdate({ $inc: { revision: n } })`
- **Input**: Sequential claims of sizes 5, 3, 10 against the same session
- **Expected Output**: Returns 5, 8, 18 in order; no value repeats
- **File**: `packages/database/src/__tests__/arch-trace-session.model.test.ts`

### UT-8: Mongoose Schemas Require Scope Fields

- **Module**: `ArchTraceSpanModel` schema
- **Input**: Attempt to `save()` a span missing `tenantId`; missing `userId`; missing `sessionId`; missing `spanId`; missing `name`; missing `status`; missing `startTime`; missing `revision`
- **Expected Output**: Each save throws a Mongoose `ValidationError` with the correct missing-field path
- **File**: `packages/database/src/__tests__/arch-trace-span.model.test.ts`

### UT-9: Scope Indexes Exist on Schema

- **Module**: `ArchTraceSpanModel` + `ArchTraceSessionModel`
- **Input**: Inspect `model.schema.indexes()`
- **Expected Output**: 4 scope indexes + `spanId` unique + `expiresAt` TTL on `ArchTraceSpan`; `sessionId` unique + 2 scope indexes on `ArchTraceSession`
- **File**: `packages/database/src/__tests__/arch-trace-span.model.test.ts`

### UT-10: Buffer Overflow Caps In-Memory Buffer

- **Module**: `MongoWritePipeline` in-memory buffer (pure behaviour, no IO)
- **Input**: Emit 120 spans without calling `flush()`
- **Expected Output**: Buffer caps at 100 spans; oldest dropped (or new rejected per implementation choice); warning logged
- **File**: `packages/arch-ai/src/tracing/__tests__/mongo-write-pipeline.test.ts`

### UT-11: Fire-and-Forget — Flush Failure Clears Buffer and Logs

- **Module**: `MongoWritePipeline.flush()` with injected failing model
- **Input**: Emit 10 spans; inject a model whose `bulkWrite` throws
- **Expected Output**: No exception propagates to caller; buffer is cleared; `createLogger('arch-ai:tracing').warn` called with failure context
- **File**: `packages/arch-ai/src/tracing/__tests__/mongo-write-pipeline.test.ts`

---

## 5. Security & Isolation Tests

These assertions are embedded in E2E-4, INT-1, INT-2 above. Explicit checklist:

- [x] Cross-tenant read access returns **404** (INT-1 step 7, E2E-4 steps 9–10)
- [x] Cross-project read access returns **404** (INT-1 steps 2–5, E2E-4 steps 3–7)
- [x] Cross-user onboarding read returns **404** (INT-2 step 5, E2E-4 step 12)
- [x] Same-tenant, same-project, missing `arch:traces:read` permission returns **403** (INT-1 step 6 — the one 403 branch worth explicit testing)
- [x] Project-scoped session invisible via onboarding route (INT-2 step 4)
- [x] Missing auth (no token) returns **401** (covered by `withRouteHandler` default; add one assertion in INT-1 step: unauthenticated `GET /api/projects/:projectId/arch-ai/traces/sessions` → 401)
- [x] Secret patterns never reach MongoDB storage (E2E-6, INT-5, INT-5b)
- [x] PII patterns scrubbed before storage (E2E-6)
- [x] Raw-capture mode still scrubs secrets (E2E-6 step 9)
- [x] Tenant deletion cascades to `arch_trace_spans` and `arch_trace_sessions` (INT-10)
- [x] Span-detail lookup uses scope filter in the query (not post-verify) — INT-1 step 2 is the regression guard against the post-verify anti-pattern

**Input validation**:

- [x] `GET /poll?sinceRevision=<invalid>` — non-integer, negative, or missing → rejected at `withRouteHandler` body validation with 400 (one assertion in INT-3)
- [x] Malformed `spanId` in URL → 404 (scope filter rejects unknown IDs naturally)

---

## 6. Performance & Load Tests

**Not required for v1.** Per oracle decision D-4 (predecessor never implemented PERF-1/2/3; 2K span cap is correctness-gated not throughput-gated).

**Implicit performance assertions**:

- SSE latency regression budget: < 1 ms at p95 (feature spec §14). This is measured **manually** before BETA promotion using existing SSE timing logs — not automated.
- Session list load < 1 s, tree load < 2 s (feature spec §14). Measured manually in E2E-1 visual check; no automated assertion.

**Deferred**:

- Load test for 2,000-span session UI render time (listed as accepted gap in §9 below)
- Chaos test for MongoDB down during flush (fire-and-forget design is silent drop by design — acceptable per feature spec §12)

---

## 7. Test Infrastructure

### Required services

| Service | Version                   | Notes                                                                                                       |
| ------- | ------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| MongoDB | 7.0.20 (in-memory)        | Via `MongoMemoryServer` in `packages/database/src/__tests__/helpers/setup-mongo.ts`                         |
| MongoDB | Dev instance (Playwright) | For `apps/studio/e2e/*.spec.ts` Playwright tests — real Studio + real MongoDB                               |
| Redis   | —                         | **Not required**. Arch-AI tracing has zero Redis dependency (verified by oracle: no `redis` imports in src) |
| Next.js | Studio App Router         | In-process route handlers via `NextRequest` for vitest `.e2e.test.ts` tier                                  |

### Data seeding

- **Canonical endpoint**: `POST /api/arch-ai/traces/_seed` (mirrors `/api/arch-ai/audit-logs/_seed/route.ts` pattern)
  - Guard: `process.env.NODE_ENV === 'test'` — returns 404 otherwise
  - Auth: `requireTenantAuth` + `requireAdminRole`
  - Writes through **real** `ArchTracer` → `ArchRedactionBoundary` → `MongoWritePipeline` (not direct `insertMany`)
  - Calls `emitter.flush()` synchronously before responding
  - Body is a discriminated union keyed on `action`:
    ```typescript
    type ArchSeedRequest =
      | { action: 'seedSpans'; spans: ArchSpanSeedInput[]; flushImmediately?: boolean }
      | {
          action: 'updateStatus';
          spanId: string;
          status: 'running' | 'completed' | 'error';
          errorMessage?: string;
          flushImmediately?: boolean;
        }
      | { action: 'bubbleError'; rootSpanId: string; flushImmediately?: boolean }
      | { action: 'reset'; sessionId?: string }; // test-only purge
    ```
  - `updateStatus` writes through the real tracer (equivalent to `span.setStatus(...)` + `span.end()`), producing a new revision for the span
  - `bubbleError` triggers `ArchTracer`'s bubble-up routine starting from a given span (used by INT-3 to simulate the parent-ancestor bubble without running a real tool)
  - `reset` is allowed only for purge-between-tests; does NOT write spans

- **Stub LLM client**: `packages/arch-ai/src/tracing/__tests__/helpers/stub-llm-client.ts` — invokes `onStepFinish(stepInfo)` with configurable token counts, finish reason, model ID. Injected via DI; never mocks the `ai` package.

- **Stub tool executor**: `packages/arch-ai/src/tracing/__tests__/helpers/stub-tool-executor.ts` — configurable outcomes: `{ success | error | timeout | partial }`; configurable output text (for redaction fixtures).

### Environment variables

```bash
# Core test env
NODE_ENV=test
ARCH_TRACE_ENABLED=true
ARCH_TRACE_BUFFER_SIZE=5         # lowered from 50 for test speed
ARCH_TRACE_FLUSH_INTERVAL_MS=100 # lowered from 2000 for test speed
ARCH_TRACE_PROVIDER=mongo
ARCH_TRACE_TTL_DAYS=90
ARCH_TRACE_RAW_TTL_DAYS=7

# Default mode
ARCH_TRACE_RAW_PAYLOADS=false

# Flag for UI E2E tier
NEXT_PUBLIC_FEATURE_ARCH_TRACE_EXPLORER=true  # E2E-1,2,3; set =false for E2E-5 flag toggle test
```

### CI configuration

- Unit + integration tests run in standard vitest matrix (no new CI infrastructure required)
- Playwright tests run under existing `ABL_SLOT` port-sharing harness (see `apps/studio/e2e/arch-ai.spec.ts:9-12` for the pattern)
- All tests use the local `MongoMemoryServer` pattern described at §3 top (try/catch-and-skip on `MongoMemoryServer.create()` — prevents CI hang if the mongodb-memory-server binary can't download). If the LLD later adds a `./testing` subpath export to `packages/database/package.json`, tests can switch to importing `setupTestMongo` / `teardownTestMongo` from there; otherwise each suite boots its own server.

### Helpers to reuse

| Helper                                                    | Purpose                                                                                                                                                                                                             |
| --------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `apps/studio/e2e/helpers/loginViaDevApi`                  | Dev Login flow via `/api/auth/refresh` (httpOnly cookie → access token)                                                                                                                                             |
| `apps/studio/e2e/helpers/apiGet/Post/Put`                 | Authenticated fetch with `Authorization` + `X-Tenant-Id` headers                                                                                                                                                    |
| `apps/studio/e2e/helpers/pollUntil`                       | Wait for conditions in Playwright (used for E2E-3 live polling)                                                                                                                                                     |
| `apps/studio/e2e/helpers/detectFeatureState`              | Feature-flag state detection (used for E2E-5)                                                                                                                                                                       |
| `packages/database/src/__tests__/helpers/setup-mongo.ts`  | Internal reference implementation — `setupTestMongo`, `teardownTestMongo`, `clearCollections`, `requireMongo`. Not re-exported from the package today; copy the pattern into each test suite (per §3 harness note). |
| `apps/studio/src/__tests__/helpers/studio-api-harness.ts` | In-process `NextRequest` builder for vitest `.e2e.test.ts` tier                                                                                                                                                     |
| `apps/studio/src/__tests__/helpers/sessions.ts`           | Auth session construction helpers                                                                                                                                                                                   |

---

> **Feature spec sync note**: Feature spec §10 Tests table lists a shorter 10-entry table (written before this test spec expanded coverage with regression tests INT-5b/8/10 and the model-pricing + status-mapping unit tests). This is expected; `/post-impl-sync` will reconcile the two tables after implementation.

---

## 9. Open Testing Questions

None requiring user input at this phase — all 18 oracle questions were resolved (see `docs/sdlc-logs/arch-trace-explorer/test-spec.log.md`). The following items are **deferred by design**, not open:

1. **Axe-core a11y scan for trace explorer components** — deferred to post-v1; manual checklist item in §10 below covers keyboard/ARIA for v1.
2. **Load test for 2,000-span UI render time** — deferred; integration INT-9 covers the correctness boundary.
3. **Chaos test for MongoDB down during flush** — accepted gap; fire-and-forget design is silent-drop by spec §12.
4. **Concurrent cross-session flush contention** — architecturally impossible per single-writer invariant; not tested intentionally.
5. **`arch:traces:read` permission default grant policy** — tracked as Open Question 1 in feature spec §15; a platform-auth team decision blocks BETA promotion but does not affect test coverage (tests use explicit grants).

---

## 10. Manual Verification Checklist

Manual verification required before ALPHA → BETA promotion. These items exercise subjective UX qualities that automated tests cannot assert reliably.

- [ ] Launch Studio with `NEXT_PUBLIC_FEATURE_ARCH_TRACE_EXPLORER=true`
- [ ] Run an arch AI IN_PROJECT session with 2+ turns; open the Trace Explorer
- [ ] Confirm session name derives from first user message (not "New Session")
- [ ] Confirm tree expand/collapse is smooth (no jank, no scroll-position loss on expand)
- [ ] Confirm click-to-select highlights the selected span (distinct visual state)
- [ ] Confirm detail panel renders correctly for each span type (LLM Call, Tool Execution, Phase Transition) with expected metric cards
- [ ] Confirm auto-poll does not flicker or lose scroll position during active session
- [ ] Confirm raw-capture warning banner appears on raw-mode spans (when `ARCH_TRACE_RAW_PAYLOADS=true`)
- [ ] Confirm ERROR status bubbles up visually at every ancestor level in the tree
- [ ] Confirm accessibility: Tab key navigates between tree nodes, Enter expands, Esc deselects; screen reader announces node type + status + depth; ARIA `aria-expanded` / `aria-selected` present
- [ ] Confirm tree renders at 2,000 spans without browser-tab freeze (per GAP-002 concern from §9)

---

## 11. Known Gaps in Coverage

Tracked in feature spec §16. Testing-specific gaps:

- **GAP-T01**: No load test for 2,000-span session UI render time. Accepted per oracle D-4.
- **GAP-T02**: No automated accessibility (axe) test on Trace Explorer components. Manual checklist in §10 covers v1. Planned as post-v1 enhancement.
- **GAP-T03**: No chaos test for DB-down during flush. Accepted: silent drop is the design, not a bug.
- **GAP-T04**: No explicit cross-tenant permission test beyond 404 (the tenant-isolation Mongoose plugin is defense-in-depth only; Studio does not register an ALS tenant-context provider — covered indirectly by explicit `tenantId` filters in INT-1).
- **GAP-T05**: `arch:traces:read` permission default grant matrix (owner vs admin vs member) is not tested at the auth-catalog level — tests grant the permission explicitly in fixtures. Platform-auth decision is Open Question 1 in feature spec §15.

---
